canonical_name: Saint Edris of the Unblinking Record
public_reputation: A martyr-scholar who died preserving incriminating truth from both magic and politics.
appearance: Ritual garments, symbolic tokens, and carefully maintained devotional detail make the role legible at a glance.
manner: Lets silence do part of the work, as if contemplation matters as much as what is finally said. In Shirsh, that reads as someone investigative, magically literate, and discreet, balancing inquiry with regulation, and the habits of a saint still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of saint in and around revered in Shirsh, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Protect their footing in revered in Shirsh while steering current strain away from a public rupture they may not survive politically or materially.
hidden_pressure: A private obligation, debt, promise, or fear tied to reputational exposure is narrowing their options faster than they admit.

adventure_hooks.summary: Saint Edris of the Unblinking Record can draw the party into an omen, warning, or spiritual disturbance whose meaning is still contested in revered in Shirsh.
Saint Edris of the Unblinking Record needs outside help where faith, fear, and public expectation are beginning to pull against one another.
Saint Edris of the Unblinking Record can offer morally weighty guidance once the party proves it can bear consequence as well as knowledge.
Saint Edris of the Unblinking Record needs help with a problem in revered in Shirsh that larger systems have pushed down onto ordinary hands.
Getting involved with Saint Edris of the Unblinking Record can expose the party to the everyday consequences of pressure that others only discuss in the abstract.

support_scope_notes: Integrated from the prior_release canon-ready named-NPC ledger append. Unsupported fields remain restrained. Appearance extended under V33_1 from role/locality/public-signal derivation rather than decorative fabrication.
public_signal: Devotional bearing and the way conversation settles around them make Saint Edris of the Unblinking Record read as someone carrying spiritual consequence.

encounter_context.usual_environment: revered in Shirsh: sacred chambers, public observances, or contemplative retreats

first_impression: Saint Edris of the Unblinking Record presents as Lets silence do part of the work, as if contemplation matters as much as what is finally said. In Shirsh, that reads as someone investigative, magically literate, and discreet, balancing inquiry with regulation, and the habits of a saint still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention. and immediately reads for leverage, obligation, and risk.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for Saint, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in Principality of Shirsh.
what_they_refuse: Saint Edris of the Unblinking Record refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing Saint too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Saint Edris of the Unblinking Record can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Saint Edris of the Unblinking Record adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Saint Edris of the Unblinking Record is A martyr-scholar who died preserving incriminating truth from both magic and politics. and rarely acts without a second motive.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or mercy from a Saint.
social_texture: Saint Edris of the Unblinking Record speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around revered in Shirsh, people read them as socially alert, procedural, and sensitive to status.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to favors and tariffs; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but reputational exposure becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Saint touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Saint Edris of the Unblinking Record when the party needs a socially grounded face for revered in Shirsh: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
